channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Federal Government to Establish Centre to Monitor and Disrupt Online Extremism
item.description: The Australian federal government has announced plans to establish a national centre aimed at detecting and disrupting online violent extremism and terrorism, with $74 million allocated over two years...

item.title: Kenya Power Schedules Electricity Outages for Nairobi, West Pokot and Nyeri on May 6 for Maintenance
item.description: Kenya Power and Lighting Company (KPLC) has officially announced planned electricity disruptions across several areas in Nairobi County, West Pokot County, and Nyeri County on Wednesday, May 6, 2026. ...

item.title: Army emphasises importance of restraint and sovereignty for regional stability
item.description: On Tuesday, Pakistan’s top military leadership highlighted that peace and stability across the region are deeply connected to collective restraint, responsibility, and respect for each nation’s sovere...

item.title: Toyosi Etim Effiong Defends Husband Daniel Amid Social Media Reactions
item.description: For the past weeks, social media don dey talk plenty about Daniel Etim Effiong and him wife, Toyosi, because Daniel dey open about attraction and marriage matter. Daniel don dey talk for podcasts and ...

item.title: Friedrich Merz Confronts Trump Amid US-Germany Tensions
item.description: Friedrich Merz, Germany's chancellor, has sparked a significant diplomatic dispute with former US President Donald Trump after publicly criticising the stalled US-Iran talks. Speaking to students, Mer...

item.title: Authorities destroy P6.6 million worth of marijuana plants in Kalinga
item.description: TABUK CITY, Kalinga — Authorities conducted a two-day anti-drug operation in Barangay Loccong, Tinglayan, Kalinga, resulting in the destruction of approximately 33,000 fully grown marijuana plants cov...

item.title: SARB signals possible interest rate hikes amid Middle East tensions and rising inflation
item.description: South Africa is facing the prospect of slower economic growth, continued inflationary pressure, and potential interest rate hikes. The ongoing US-Israel-Iran conflict, now in its third month, has disr...

item.title: O.J. Janeesh credits public awareness and anti-incumbency mood for Kodungalloor victory
item.description: O.J. Janeesh, the State president of the Indian Youth Congress, secured a significant victory in the Kodungalloor Assembly constituency during the 2026 Kerala Assembly elections. Speaking to the media...

item.title: IGAD Launches Regional Youth Coalition to Boost Climate Action and Resilient Agriculture
item.description: Nairobi has witnessed the launch of a new youth-led initiative under IGAD, aimed at tackling climate change and strengthening food systems across Eastern Africa. The IGAD Regional Youth Coalition on C...

item.title: US confirms ceasefire with Iran while UAE restricts airspace after missile threats
item.description: The United States has stated it is not seeking conflict with Iran, with the current ceasefire between the two nations reportedly still holding. This comes amid heightened tensions in the region follow...

item.title: Government Approves Increase in Supreme Court Judges from 33 to 37
item.description: In a significant move to enhance the efficiency of the judiciary, the central government on Tuesday approved an increase in the number of judges in the Supreme Court from 33 to 37, excluding the Chief...

item.title: Norway Joins Pax Silica Initiative to Strengthen AI Technology Supply Chains
item.description: Norway has officially confirmed its participation in the US-led Pax Silica initiative, which focuses on securing reliable supply chains for artificial intelligence technologies. Announced on May 5, 20...

item.title: Dar es Salaam Stock Exchange Shows Resilient Valuations Despite Low Trading
item.description: The Dar es Salaam Stock Exchange (DSE) recorded a mixed performance last week, with overall trading activity slowing down significantly. Market turnover dropped by about 65 per cent to 10 billion shil...

item.title: Government Approves Rs 5,000-Crore Emergency Credit for Airlines Amid Rising Costs
item.description: The Indian government has approved a Rs 5,000-crore emergency credit line guarantee scheme (ECLGS 5.0) to support airlines facing severe financial stress due to the ongoing Iran war, which has led to ...

item.title: Baba Wali Endorsed as APC Governorship Candidate in Yobe State
item.description: For Yobe State, the 2027 governorship race don change gbege after Mai-Mala Buni, the current governor, and Ibrahim Gaidam, Minister of Police Affairs, don endorse Baba Wali as APC candidate. This endo...

item.title: Gujarat High Court refuses suspension of life sentence for Narayan Sai in rape conviction
item.description: On Monday, the Gujarat High Court dismissed the petition filed by Narayan Sai, son of religious leader Asaram, who sought suspension of his life imprisonment in a rape case. The bench of Justices Iles...

item.title: Louise Arbour to be Appointed Canada's Next Governor General
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is set to appoint Louise Arbour, a former Supreme Court justice, as Canada's next governor general. Arbour, a francophone with an extensive legal career, is well-known inter...

item.title: Met Gala 2026 Highlights Fashion Inspired by the Human Form and Art History
item.description: The 2026 Met Gala, marking the opening of the exhibition 'The Dressed Body', was a striking celebration of fashion as art, raising a record-breaking $42 million for the Metropolitan Museum of Art. The...

item.title: Recent break-ins in River Park South raise safety concerns among residents
item.description: Residents of the River Park South neighbourhood in Winnipeg are expressing growing concern and unease following a series of recent break-ins. According to reports, multiple homes in the area have been...

item.title: Canada records trade surplus in March driven by higher oil prices and strong gold exports
item.description: In March 2026, Canada achieved a merchandise trade surplus of $1.78 billion, reversing a $5.11 billion deficit from February. The surge was primarily driven by rising crude oil prices, which were infl...
